quiz.title: Unit 12 Quiz 9, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Gas Stoichiometry
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Gas Stoichiometry, Volume-Volume Relationships, Mass-Volume Relationships, Ideal Gas Law in Reactions

questions.question:
What volume of H₂ at STP is produced from 5.4 g Al?
2Al + 6HCl → 2AlCl₃ + 3H₂
questions.explanation:
Molar mass Al = 27 g/mol
Moles Al = 5.4/27 = 0.20 mol
Mole ratio Al:H₂ = 2:3, so moles H₂ = 0.20 × (3/2) = 0.30 mol
At STP, volume H₂ = 0.30 × 22.4 = 6.72 L

questions.question:
The decomposition of 12.25 g KClO₃ produces how many liters O₂ at STP?
2KClO₃ → 2KCl + 3O₂
questions.explanation:
Molar mass KClO₃ = 122.5 g/mol
Moles KClO₃ = 12.25/122.5 = 0.10 mol
Mole ratio KClO₃:O₂ = 2:3, so moles O₂ = 0.10 × (3/2) = 0.15 mol
Volume O₂ at STP = 0.15 × 22.4 = 3.36 L

questions.question: How many moles of CO₂ are in 11.2 L at STP?
questions.explanation: At STP, 22.4 L = 1 mol, so 11.2 L = 0.50 mol

questions.question:
The combustion of 1.0 L C₃H₈ at STP requires how many liters O₂ at STP?
C₃H₈ + 5O₂ → 3CO₂ + 4H₂O
questions.explanation:
At same T and P, volume ratio = mole ratio
Volume O₂ = 1.0 L × 5 = 5.0 L

questions.question:
How many grams of Na are needed to produce 5.6 L H₂ at STP?
2Na + 2H₂O → 2NaOH + H₂
questions.explanation:
At STP, 5.6 L H₂ = 5.6/22.4 = 0.25 mol H₂
Mole ratio Na:H₂ = 2:1, so moles Na = 0.25 × 2 = 0.50 mol
Molar mass Na = 23 g/mol, mass Na = 0.50 × 23 = 11.5 g

questions.question:
What volume of NO at 25°C and 1 atm is produced from 3.0 mol NH₃?
4NH₃ + 5O₂ → 4NO + 6H₂O
questions.explanation:
Mole ratio NH₃:NO = 4:4 = 1:1, so moles NO = 3.0 mol
At 25°C (298 K) and 1 atm, V = nRT/P = 3.0 × 0.0821 × 298 = 73.4 L ≈ 73.9 L

questions.question:
How many liters of SO₂ at STP are produced from 16.0 g Cu₂S?
Cu₂S + O₂ → 2Cu + SO₂
questions.explanation:
Molar mass Cu₂S = (2×63.55) + 32.06 = 159.16 g/mol
Moles Cu₂S = 16.0/159.16 = 0.1005 mol
Mole ratio Cu₂S:SO₂ = 1:1, so moles SO₂ = 0.1005 mol
Volume SO₂ at STP = 0.1005 × 22.4 = 2.25 L

questions.question:
How many liters of CO₂ at STP are produced from burning 16.0 g CH₄?
CH₄ + 2O₂ → CO₂ + 2H₂O
questions.explanation:
Molar mass CH₄ = 16 g/mol
Moles CH₄ = 16.0/16 = 1.0 mol
Mole ratio CH₄:CO₂ = 1:1, so moles CO₂ = 1.0 mol
Volume CO₂ at STP = 1.0 × 22.4 = 22.4 L

questions.question:
What mass of CaCO₃ is needed to produce 11.2 L CO₂ at STP?
CaCO₃ → CaO + CO₂
questions.explanation:
At STP, 11.2 L CO₂ = 0.50 mol CO₂
Mole ratio CaCO₃:CO₂ = 1:1, so moles CaCO₃ = 0.50 mol
Molar mass CaCO₃ = 100 g/mol, mass = 0.50 × 100 = 50.0 g

questions.question: The property of compressibility in gases is due to ___________
questions.explanation: Gases are highly compressible because there are large empty spaces between gas particles. When pressure is applied, these particles can be pushed closer together, reducing the volume significantly. This is not possible in liquids or solids where particles are already closely packed.

questions.question: When electricity flows through _______, it produces a red light.
questions.explanation: Neon gas produces a characteristic red-orange glow when electricity passes through it in a discharge tube. This is the principle behind neon signs. Other gases produce different colors: argon produces lavender/blue, helium produces pink/orange, and krypton produces white/green.

questions.question:
The reaction of 4.0 L H₂ with excess N₂ produces how many liters NH₃ at same T and P?
N₂ + 3H₂ → 2NH₃
questions.explanation:
At same T and P, volume ratio = mole ratio
Volume NH₃ = 4.0 L H₂ × (2 mol NH₃/3 mol H₂) = 4.0 × 2/3 = 2.67 L

questions.question: How many moles of O₂ are in 44.8 L at 25°C and 2.0 atm?
The partial pressure of O₂ in air is approximately 0.21 atm. How many liters of O₂ at STP are in 100 L air at 1 atm?
questions.explanation: Volume of O₂ in air = 100 L × 0.21 = 21 L (at same T and P)

questions.question:
How many grams of Zn are needed to produce 10.0 L H₂ at 25°C and 1.5 atm?
Zn + 2HCl → ZnCl₂ + H₂
questions.explanation:
T = 25 + 273 = 298 K
n = PV/RT = (1.5 × 10.0)/(0.0821 × 298) = 15.0/24.47 = 0.613 mol H₂
Mole ratio Zn:H₂ = 1:1, so moles Zn = 0.613 mol
Molar mass Zn = 65.4 g/mol
Mass Zn = 0.613 × 65.4 = 40.1 g

scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review volume ratios and Ideal Gas Law applications.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on mole ratios and gas volume conversions.
scoreMessages.0: Please review gas stoichiometry, molar volume at STP, and the Ideal Gas Law.
